arguments.task: Research Neo4j using official and first-party sources where possible. Find evidence for: 1) one-line description of Neo4j, 2) public API/docs surface available today from https://neo4j.com/docs/api and adjacent official docs, including whether APIs are REST, GraphQL, both, or none, and breadth, 3) authentication methods for the documented API surfaces (only if explicitly documented), 4) pricing/access or signup requirements for using the relevant APIs/services, 5) any official or first-party rate limit notes, 6) whether Neo4j has an official or clearly existing MCP server/integration, preferring official docs, GitHub from Neo4j, or MCP registry if available. Prioritize official developer docs, auth docs, pricing pages, support docs, changelogs, GitHub org pages, and product docs. Avoid blog posts unless necessary. Return concise structured findings with exact source URLs per claim, and note any ambiguity.

input.thought: Launch constrained browser research on official Neo4j sources and MCP evidence.

raw_result.description: Graph database and graph analytics platform for building and querying connected data applications.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Neo4j Query API docs show HTTP authentication via Authorization header using Basic auth and Bearer auth; auth can be disabled for local development, and SSO is also documented for Aura/console contexts, but the evidence gathered is strongest for Basic and Bearer on the Query API.
raw_result.gating_reason: Neo4j provides publicly accessible documentation, downloadable Community Edition, self-serve Aura signup/pricing, and public docs for multiple application interfaces without partner approval.

api_surface.breadth: Official docs expose multiple surfaces: HTTP-based Query API, deprecated HTTP API, Bolt protocol drivers, and a GraphQL library/API layer for application development. The browser run also found official MCP documentation under Neo4j docs, indicating additional agent-oriented integration surface.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official Neo4j documentation includes an MCP section at neo4j.com/docs/mcp/current/, which is strong evidence that a Neo4j MCP offering exists.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No explicit API rate-limit policy was verified from the gathered evidence. Neo4j Aura/platform limits may exist in separate docs, but they were not confirmed from terminal output here.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs indicate the legacy HTTP API is deprecated in favor of the Query API. Neo4j also maintains first-party MCP documentation, which is a strong signal for agent/toolkit relevance. Public docs also point developers to Bolt, HTTP/Query API, and GraphQL-oriented application paths.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Some fields remain partially ambiguous from the gathered evidence only: exact one-line product wording, whether API surface should be classified strictly as REST versus mixed HTTP/protocol plus GraphQL, whether SSO should count as an auth method for toolkit purposes, and whether a first-party documented rate-limit page should change access/buildability nuances.

Public docs also point developers to Bolt, HTTP/Query API, and GraphQL-oriented application paths.
